name: BB:BehaviorDefinition: Abuse of Findstr
notes:
This Building Block detects abuse of findstr for evasion. Adversaries can use findstr to hide their artifacts or search specific strings and evade defense mechanism.

Covered from SIGMA Rule.

Original Author: Furkan CALISKAN, @caliskanfurkan_, @oscd_initiative, Nasreddine Bencherchali
SIGMA Title: Abusing Findstr for Defense Evasion
SIGMA Description: Attackers can use findstr to hide their artifacts or search specific strings and evade defense mechanism


Confidence: LOW

This rule may be covered under the Detection Rule License: https://ibm.biz/SigmaDetectionRuleLicense
bb_references: BB:BehaviorDefinition: Abuse of Findstr on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
conditions_text: when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Process Creation Success
when the event matches Process Name (custom) is any of [findstr.exe or FINDSTR.EXE]

when the event matches (LOWER("Command") LIKE '%findstr%') and (((LOWER("Command") LIKE '% /v %' or LOWER("Command") LIKE '% -v %') and (LOWER("Command") LIKE '% /l %' or LOWER("Command") LIKE '% -l %')) or ((LOWER("Command") LIKE '% /s %' or LOWER("Command") LIKE '% -s %') and (LOWER("Command") LIKE '% /i %' or LOWER("Command") LIKE '% -i %'))) AQL filter query
TD_list: APPLY BB:BehaviorDefinition: Abuse of Findstr on events which are detected by the LOCAL system
AND when the event(s) were detected by one or more of Microsoft Windows Security Event Log
AND when the event category for the event is one of the following System.Process Creation Success
AND when the event matches Process Name (custom) is any of [findstr.exe or FINDSTR.EXE]

AND when the event matches (LOWER("Command") LIKE '%findstr%') and (((LOWER("Command") LIKE '% /v %' or LOWER("Command") LIKE '% -v %') and (LOWER("Command") LIKE '% /l %' or LOWER("Command") LIKE '% -l %')) or ((LOWER("Command") LIKE '% /s %' or LOWER("Command") LIKE '% -s %') and (LOWER("Command") LIKE '% /i %' or LOWER("Command") LIKE '% -i %'))) AQL filter query
